text: I find myself wandering halls and spaces bathed in blue and violet light, surrounded by symbols of research and industry. The world outside is distant; inside, the environment feels both futuristic and isolated. Not another soul crosses my path. My mind drifts between curiosity—what innovations might be created here?—and an unsettled longing for warmth and life. The philosophical question arises: does innovation thrive more in solitude or in shared existence?
